description: Running a global build/test command for Go instead of the go toolchain (go build/test/vet) misses the project-local config and reports misleading results.
body: Failure mode: a generic root-level command is run for Go work instead of the go toolchain (go build/test/vet), so project-local configuration, lockfiles, and scripts are ignored and the output does not reflect the real build. Detection signals: the command ignores the project manifest; results differ from CI; tooling versions or flags do not match the repo. Do not run a global or default command when the project declares its own Go toolchain. Safer fallback: detect and run the go toolchain (go build/test/vet) from the project root, verify the version matches the repo, and record the exact command and exit code.
